subject: Geology
title: Fracking and Shale Gas
description: A quick, practical guide to Fracking and Shale Gas.

hook.text: Trapped gas in tight rock once seemed unreachable. Then engineers learned to crack the rock open from inside.

buildup.text: Hydraulic fracturing pumps high-pressure fluid into shale to crack it open, releasing oil and gas from tiny pores.

discovery.text: The US shale boom made America a top oil and gas producer. Fracking unlocked reserves once considered worthless.

twist.text: Fracking raises concerns about water use, contamination, and induced earthquakes from wastewater injection.

climax.text: Fracking changed global energy markets overnight. It shows how technology can rewrite resource geology.

punchline.text: Cracking rock cracked open the energy map.

quiz.question: What does hydraulic fracturing do?
options: Melts rock with heat to release gas
Pumps high-pressure fluid to crack rock and release trapped gas
